vulnerabilities.description: The X-Content-Type-Options header is not set on the server, allowing potential mime-sniffing attacks.

fix.law: Set X-Content-Type-Options header to 'nosniff' to prevent mime-sniffing attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: The X-Frame-Options header is not set, making the site vulnerable to clickjacking attacks.

fix.law: Set X-Frame-Options header to 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' to prevent clickjacking attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: No Content Security Policy header is set, which can help prevent cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.

fix.law: Implement a Content Security Policy to mitigate XSS attacks.

vulnerabilities.flaw: HTTP Strict Transport Security Not Enforced
vulnerabilities.description: The site is not enforcing HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS), leaving it vulnerable to SSL-stripping attacks.

fix.law: Enable HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) to ensure all communication is over HTTPS.

vulnerabilities.description: There is no Subresource Integrity (SRI) attribute set for external scripts, making the site susceptible to script injection attacks.

fix.law: Implement Subresource Integrity (SRI) for external scripts to verify their integrity and mitigate script injection attacks.

flaws.description: The website is missing key security headers like Content-Security-Policy (CSP), X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and X-XSS-Protection which can help protect against various types of attacks such as cross-site scripting (XSS), clickjacking, and MIME sniffing.

fix.action: Implement the necessary security headers in the website's HTTP responses. For example, set a Content-Security-Policy header to restrict the sources from which certain types of content can be loaded, set X-Content-Type-Options to nosniff, set X-Frame-Options to deny or sameorigin to prevent clickjacking attacks, and set X-XSS-Protection to 1; mode=block to enable XSS protection in modern browsers.

flaws.description: The website is serving mixed content (both HTTP and HTTPS) which can lead to security vulnerabilities. This can result in attacks like man-in-the-middle (MITM) where an attacker can tamper with the non-secure content.

fix.action: Ensure that all content on the website is served over HTTPS. Update all resources, links, and scripts to use secure HTTPS connections.

flaws.description: The website may be using outdated software, plugins, or libraries which could contain known vulnerabilities that can be exploited by attackers.

fix.action: Regularly update and patch all software, plugins, and libraries to the latest versions to mitigate known vulnerabilities. Implement a system to monitor and update software regularly.

flaws.description: The website may have weak session management mechanisms in place, making it vulnerable to session fixation, session hijacking, and other attacks.

fix.action: Ensure that session tokens are securely generated, transmitted over secure channels (HTTPS), and invalidated after logout or a certain period of inactivity. Implement mechanisms like secure httponly and secure flags for session cookies.

flaws.description: The website may be vulnerable to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks where an attacker can inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users.

fix.action: Implement input validation and encoding to prevent user input from being interpreted as code. Use Content Security Policy (CSP) to restrict the sources from which content can be loaded.

vulnerabilities.description: The website is using outdated software which may contain known security vulnerabilities that can be exploited by attackers.
vulnerabilities.fix: Update all software components (e.g. web server, CMS, plugins) to their latest versions to patch known security vulnerabilities.

vulnerabilities.description: The website is missing important security headers that can help protect against common web vulnerabilities.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement security headers such as Content Security Policy (CSP), HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS), X-Frame-Options, and X-XSS-Protection to enhance the security of the website.

vulnerabilities.description: The website has a weak SSL/TLS configuration leaving it vulnerable to attacks like man-in-the-middle.
vulnerabilities.fix: Update SSL/TLS configuration to enforce strong encryption algorithms, use secure ciphers, enable Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS), and disable deprecated protocols (e.g. TLS 1.0/1.1).

vulnerabilities.description: The website may be exposing sensitive information (e.g. credentials, session tokens) in clear text or unsafely storing them.
vulnerabilities.fix: Ensure that sensitive data is encrypted both in transit and at rest. Implement secure storage mechanisms like hashing and encryption for sensitive information.

vulnerabilities.description: The website is vulnerable to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks which can lead to client-side code execution.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement input validation, output encoding, and proper escaping of user-generated content to prevent XSS attacks. Use security controls like Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate XSS risks.

vulnerabilities.description: The website is missing important security headers which can help protect against various attacks such as XSS, clickjacking, and MIME sniffing.

fix.description: Implement relevant security headers such as Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and Strict-Transport-Security in the website's HTTP responses.
fix.example: Add 'Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' www.google-analytics.com' header to enable Content Security Policy.

vulnerabilities.description: The website may be running outdated software with known security vulnerabilities which can be exploited by attackers.

fix.description: Regularly update the website's software, including the CMS, plugins, and server software, to the latest secure versions.
fix.example: Update WordPress to the latest version to patch known vulnerabilities.

vulnerabilities.description: The website may be vulnerable to XSS attacks, allowing attackers to inject malicious scripts into the website.

fix.description: Implement input validation and output encoding to prevent XSS attacks. Use appropriate libraries or frameworks that automatically escape user input.
fix.example: Use htmlspecialchars() function in PHP to encode user input before displaying it on the website.

vulnerabilities.description: The website may be exposing sensitive data such as credentials or personal information without proper protection.

fix.description: Ensure sensitive data is encrypted in transit and at rest. Implement proper access controls and never store sensitive information in client-side storage.
fix.example: Use HTTPS and TLS to encrypt data in transit, and encrypt sensitive data in the database using algorithms like AES.

vulnerabilities.description: Directory listing is enabled on the web server, potentially disclosing sensitive information about the website's directory structure.

fix.description: Disable directory listing on the web server to prevent unauthorized access to directory contents.
fix.example: Configure web server settings to disable directory listing, such as Options -Indexes in Apache configuration.
